PG Programme in
Sustainability & Business Management

A programme designed to launch your career at the intersection of
sustainability, business, and impact.

Apply Now

PG Programme in Sustainability & Business Management

Format

Full Time

On-campus

Commencement Date

August '26

Applications Open

Eligibility

Bachelor’s Degree

Or equivalent with 0-3 years of experience

Duration

12 Months

With live projects, internships & industry-led sustainability sprints

Round 2 Starting Soon Apply Now
  • Highlights
  • Curriculum & Faculty
  • Admissions & Fees

Why Sustainability, Why Now?

India’s 2030 climate targets and 2070 net-zero greenhouse gas goal have turned climate action into a core business priority. Much of India’s emissions come from power, manufacturing, transport, construction, and supply chains, so companies now need to measure carbon, cut it credibly, and report it transparently.

With BRSR/ESG expectations rising, organisations need job-ready talent that can
do carbon accounting, climate-risk analysis, & practical decarbonisation planning. This programme builds those capabilities through hands-on tools, real industry cases, & outcome-focused projects aligned to what companies are hiring for.

Designed Around the 17 UN Sustainable
Development Goals

The Masters’ Union Edge in
Sustainability

Build a career in sustainability by gaining real industry
experience to move into ESG and climate-tech roles.

Learn from Global
Sustainability & Business Leaders

Study with experts from NASA, global climate funds, Fortune
500 ESG teams, climate-tech ventures & policy institutions.

1

Practitioners from NASA, climate funds, ESG teams

2

Policymakers from NITI Aayog, govt researchers, activists

3

Sustainability leaders from Samsung, Airtel, Tesla

Apply Business Skills to
India’s Net-Zero Opportunity

An SDG-linked curriculum built for India’s 2030 climate targets and near-term decarbonisation.

1

Develop go-to-market strategies for sustainable products

2

Build revenue models that cater to new rules

3

Draft ESG reports aligned with SEBI BRSR standards

Experience Sustainability on a
Global Stage

Learn how SDGs translate into business action through Global Immersion Programmes (GIPs).

1

Study sustainability policy at the UN Headquarters in Geneva

2

Learn climate finance at Rabobank HQ in Amsterdam

3

Study smart-city decarbonisation in Singapore

Accelerate Your Career into the
Climate Economy

Move into industry-ready roles across ESG, climate finance, and
sustainability strategy.

1

Pathways into ESG consulting roles in EY, PwC, Deloitte

2

Careers in sustainability teams at Tata, Mahindra

3

Learn from climate-tech experts from Samsung, Green Marble

Learn from Global
Sustainability & Business Leaders

Study with experts from NASA, global climate funds, Fortune
500 ESG teams, climate-tech ventures & policy institutions.

3

3

Business Experts

Learn from sustainability leaders, climate-tech founders, & ESG strategists
from NASA, global climate funds, Fortune 500s and consulting firms.

Real Insights from
Our Faculty

Gain insights from industry leaders on how sustainability is shaping the future!

Ruchira Shukla, Founder & CEO - Green Marble

What is the VC mindset for investing in climate tech?

Pawan Raj Kumar, Partner - ZeCa Capital

Are sustainable businesses truly investable?

Dr. Bhaskar Chatterjee, Father of Indian CSR

How India made CSR mandatory and why it matters?

Tanya Singhal, Founder - SolarArise

How is India’s renewable energy market evolving?

Jatayi Talapatra, Business Sustainability Author

Can organisations thrive by caring for people & planet?

Vivek Mehra, Co-Founder - OnlyGood

How carbon reporting & AI improve sustainability decisions?

Learn by Doing
Not Just by Sitting in Lectures

Play a new role in the sustainability ecosystem each term, from ESG analyst, sustainability consultant to climate strategist.

Sustainable Dropshipping Challenge

In Just Six Weeks,
Launch a Sustainable Business

Build and scale a sustainability-focused dropshipping store
targeting ₹5–10L in GMV with real budgets and real customers.

  • Source and audit credible eco-certified products and suppliers
  • Launch a Shopify/Marketplace store and acquire 200+ paid orders
  • Optimise CAC, ROAS and repeat purchases like a D2C brand

ESG Strategy Challenge

Design Real ESG Strategies for
Forward-Looking Companies

Act as an ESG team for a listed-style company and build a
2030 roadmap inspired by leaders like Tata, Infosys or Unilever.

  • Benchmark ESG performance against Indian and global peers
  • Design net-zero, circularity and Scope 3 mitigation roadmaps
  • Model 30–40% emission cuts and ESG KPIs

The Policy Playbook Challenge

Design India’s Next Rulebook for
Sustainability Regulation

Act as national policymakers designing India’s next sustainability
rules for FMCG, fashion, e-commerce and logistics sectors.

  • Model the impact of new rules on a company like HUL, Nykaa or Blinkit
  • Redesign packaging, sourcing and logistics to cut carbon and compliance risk
  • Present a “Policy Pivot Plan” with revenue, cost and emission impacts

Waste-to-Wealth Innovation Lab

Turn Real Waste Streams into
Circular Ventures

Design ventures that turn fashion, F&B & e-commerce waste
streams into revenue, inspired by TerraCycle & Banyan Nation.

  • Map waste from CyberHub food courts, Blinkit hubs or campus hostels
  • Prototype upcycling for H&M offcuts or Zomato packaging with unit economics
  • Pitch a circular model with revenue, tCO₂e saved and jobs created

Sustainability Reporting War Room

Fix Greenwashing:
Build Audit-Ready ESG Reports

Work with messy, multi-year data from an Indian “listed-style”
company & convert it into a BRSR/Core-ready disclosure.

  • Reconcile multi-year ESG data across energy, water, waste & workforce
  • Draft key BRSR/Core sections, tables & management commentary
  • Stress-test disclosures against rating and assurance checklists

Green Supply Chain Rearchitecture Challenge

Rebuild Supply Chains
for Low-Carbon Scale

Act as an ops and sustainability team cutting supply chain
emissions by 30% for a real Indian business.

  • Baseline supplier-to-last-mile emissions for Flipkart or BigBasket
  • Redesign packaging and freight to hit 30% cuts using EVs and rail
  • Align suppliers to SBTi-style targets with contracts & scorecards

Sustainable Student Ventures

Pranjal Agrawal, Founder, Source With Solomon
Helping global buyers source authentic Indian products, focusing on sustainable and eco-friendly packaging and goods.

Jaishree Soni, Founder, Saaha
Building a luxury house that turns Indian culture, mythology, and craft into limited-edition collectible art for global buyers and collectors .

Ananya Kulshrestha , Co-founder, Mellovia
Creating stylish, non-toxic kitchenware made from natural rice husk, a durable, biodegradable alternative to plastic for conscious homes.

Pranjal Agrawal, Founder, Source With Solomon
Helping global buyers source authentic Indian products, focusing on sustainable and eco-friendly packaging and goods.

Ananya Kulshrestha , Co-founder, Mellovia
Creating stylish, non-toxic kitchenware made from natural rice husk, a durable, biodegradable alternative to plastic for conscious homes.

Life Beyond the Classroom

Get a glimpse into the energy, talent, and adventures that
shape student life beyond the classroom.

Learn with Our Expert Community

Surround yourself with ESG leaders, climate-tech founders, policy
professionals, and impact investors who are building the climate
economy. Learn in public, stress-test your ideas, and grow inside a
community that thinks in SDGs and executes like operators.

1

Apply climate, policy & ESG insights to real business problems.

2

Get 1:1 feedback from sustainability leaders and practitioners.

3

Solve SDG-linked challenges in small, cross-functional cohorts.

4

Build a portfolio of climate projects, reports & venture ideas.

5

Access AMAs, roundtables & office hours with global experts.

Reach out to the
Support Team

Email ID

pgadmissions@mastersunion.org

Mobile no.

+91 8929361765

Student Placement Experience

Download Placement Report

Archit Bhargava

Analyst, Avaana Capital

“The hands-on learning environment and Venture Capital program helped me understand how funds evaluate startups for long-term growth. The exposure to real investors and investment committees was instrumental in helping me secure my role at Avaana Capital, one of India’s leading climate-tech and sustainability-focused venture funds.”

1/6

Aashutosh Varakhedkar

Expert – ESG, Trident Group

“Masters’ Union played a crucial role in helping me secure my position at Trident. The program’s emphasis on data-driven decision-making and real-world problem solving strengthened my analytical thinking. Masters’ Union has truly been the launchpad for my transition into the ESG domain.”

2/6

Yeshvanth Suresh

Chief of Staff, Ather Energy

“While the core learnings at Masters’ Union impacted my overall knowledge, live consulting projects and building a startup from scratch significantly improved my problem-solving ability. These experiences prepared me to crack a role with Ather.”

3/6

Priyansh Sharma

Senior Analyst, Talabat (Dubai)

“I must have met and learned from 50+ CXOs on the Masters’ Union campus. Taking every outclass challenge as an opportunity to grow helped me become a better professional and ultimately secure my role at Talabat in Dubai.”

4/6

Jas Desai

Senior Associate, CEO’s Office (Transformation & Strategy), DP World (Dubai)

“Masters’ Union’s in-class and out-class learning equipped me with practical problem-solving skills directly applicable to my Transformation & Strategy role at DP World. Regular CXO interactions helped me understand how large organizations drive impact at scale.”

5/6

Yash Chhabra

Investment Associate, Waterbridge Ventures

“Masters’ Union’s Creator Challenge pushed me to build a startup and VC-focused podcast. The mentorship opportunities and exposure to founders and investors helped me develop a strong perspective on evaluating new-age businesses and secure my role at Waterbridge Ventures.”

6/6
Download Placement Report

In the News

Coverage and features highlighting Masters’ Union across leading media platforms.

World-Class Campus in the
Heart of Gurugram

Book a Campus Tour

Engage With a
Comprehensive Sustainability &
Business Curriculum

Explore modules blending management, strategy, sustainability & climate
finance, preparing you for careers in sustainability and business.

Curriculum with
Unique In Class and Out Class Combination

  • Year 1

    Learn Fundamentals of Tech and Business

  • Year 2

    Excel in New-Age Skills

  • Year 3

    Transform into a Business Leader

  • Year 4

    Get Ready for the Real World

In Class | Core Courses

Foundational Courses

  • Art of Communication - Public Speaking & Elocution

  • Introduction to Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel

  • Legalities in Business - From Compliances to Loopholes

  • Fundamentals of Accounting - Understanding Financial Statements



  • Macroeconomics - How Economy Affects Business

  • Microeconomics - Understanding the Dynamics of Demand and Supply

    • Strategic Management

  • Problem Solving and Structuring - How Leaders Make Decisions 





    • Finance and Fintech

  • Managerial Accounting - Diagnosing Business Health

    • Product Management

  • Introduction to Product Management - How to Build an App





    • Digital Marketing & Brand Management

  • Building Marketing Strategies - Getting Customers to Buy the Product

    • Data Analytics

  • Principles of Statistics - Navigating Uncertainties in Business

  • Mastering Data Visualisation - Telling a Story with Data

    • Out Class

    Dropshipping Challenge

    Students target to get 1Cr+ in revenue by building their e-commerce ventures & selling on Amazon, BlinkIt & own websites.

    Download Dropshipping Report

    Global Immersion Programme

    Get the chance to travel and explore a new country and immerse yourself in its business, community, and socioeconomics.

    Download Japan GIP Report

    Shopfloor Internship

    Work on-ground with leading D2C brands to gain real-time consumer insights and solve business challenges firsthand.

    Download Internship Report

    In Class | Core + Elective Courses

    Foundational Courses

  • Managerial Behaviour - Understanding Human Psychology

  • Introduction to Operations - Building Systems from the Ground Up

  • Introduction to HRM - Managing People and Processes




    • Strategic Management

  • Corporate Strategies - Building Competitive Advantage

  • Corporate Governance -  How should Boards Work

    • Finance and Fintech

  • Corporate Finance - Maximising Shareholder Value

  • Venture Capital & FinTech - Decoding the Future of Finance


    • Product Management

  • Design Thinking and Prototyping - Building Customer-Centric Products

  • Product Analytics - Measuring Success with Metrics

    • Digital Marketing & Brand Management

  • Digital Marketing - How Instagram and YouTube Can Get You Customers

  • Understanding Consumer Behaviour-  Decoding Consumer Decision Making

    • Data Analytics

  • Introduction to Machine Learning -1 - Understanding Algorithms behind AI

    • Out Class

    Creator Challenge

    Transform your passion into profit by mastering the art of building and monetising personal brands as Creator-preneurs.

    Download Content Creator Challenge Report

    Local Consulting Challenge

    Tackle real-world business problems faced by local companies, develop innovative solutions, and gain hands-on consulting experience.

    Corporate Internships

    From EY to Zomato, work with top companies, apply classroom knowledge to practical challenges, and build a network that can jumpstart your professional journey.

    Download Internship Report

    In Class

    Foundational Courses

  • Intro to Blockchain, Metaverse & AI - Understanding New Age Technologies

  • Public Policy and Advocacy - How Businesses work with Government

    • Strategic Management

  • The Founder's Playbook - Strategic Scenarios & Real-World Application



  • Negotiation & Financial Acumen - Crafting Win-Win Solutions 

    • Finance and Fintech

  • Valuations & Financial Modelling - Finding a Company’s Worth

  • Markets, Portfolios & Prudence - The Investment Playbook






    • Product Management

  • Product Led Growth Strategies - Scaling 10X with Product

  • Product Strategy and Innovation - How Product Managers Innovate









    • Digital Marketing & Brand Management

  • Strategic Brand Management - How to Scale a Brand

  • Content Generation and Advertising - How Advertisers Influence Customers

    • Data Analytics

  • Big Data Analytics - Crunching Numbers to Find Patterns

  • Introduction to Machine Learning -2 - Applications in Business

    • Out Class

    Industry Treks

    Participate in industry-specific tours, and get the chance to interact with investors, founders, and executives of leading companies.

    Leadership Retreats

    Learn the art of leadership through a 7-10 day offsite retreat with uncertain and ever changing environments, and get trained in complex decision making.

    Entrepreneurship Capstone

    Experience a 0-to-1 journey from ideation to pitching real VCs, and get a grant to launch your own startups.

    Download Entrepreneurship Report

    Out Class

    Industry Training

    Spend a year working alongside CXOs from top companies like KPMG, Razorpay, and McKinsey, while receiving mentorship from industry experts in your field.

    Download Internship Report

    Startup Incubation

    Take your entrepreneurial ventures to the next level. Get the support, resources, and mentorship needed to scale your startup, and refine your business model.

    Download Entrepreneurship Report

    Enabling You to Excel
    Inside and Outside the Classroom

    Fundamentals of Business & Sustainability

    Understand how sustainability is reshaping the world and learn how businesses can integrate sustainability into strategy, finance, branding, and decision-making.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How the world is moving towards sustainability?

    • Global sustainability landscape & international policy direction: Paris Agreement, COP
    • UN 2030 Agenda as the common global framework: SDGs
    • India’s sustainability trajectory vs global benchmarks: EU/US/China

    How businesses can become sustainable?

    • Ecosystem mapping: suppliers, customers, government
    • Root cause analysis: waste, emissions, compliance gaps
    • Materiality thinking and priority-setting
    • Value chain hotspots and intervention areas

    What are the global best practices in sustainability?

    • Global best practices and benchmarks
    • Sustainability as a mindset and competitive advantage
    • Case studies across sectors: H&M, Unilever, IKEA, Tesla, Google

    How to read financial statements and make business decisions?

    • Reading financial statements: P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow
    • Cash-flow–dependent decision-making: runway, working capital
    • Pricing, costs & contribution margin
    • Investment decision-making: NPV, IRR, ROI

    How to position and price sustainable brands for scale?

    • Consumer behaviour: intent vs purchase
    • Positioning & messaging for sustainable brands
    • Credible claims and trust-building: greenwashing risk
    • Pricing strategy for sustainable products: premium vs margin

    Sustainable Dropshipping Challenge:

    Launch and run a live sustainability led online business end to end. Validate a product opportunity, audit supplier claims, build the store, acquire real customers through paid marketing, manage fulfilment, and track business performance plus sustainability impact.

    Governance, Policy & ESG

    Learn how sustainability is governed, regulated, reported, and communicated—through laws, ESG frameworks, CSR, and leadership communication.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How to create accurate & reliable ESG reports?

    • Materiality assessment & stakeholder mapping
    • Key Indian and global ESG reporting frameworks: GRI, BRSR, ISSB, IFRS, CSRD
    • Third-party risk assessment: credibility, greenwashing
    • ESG dashboarding & report generation

    How to leverage government policies to build a sustainable business in India?

    • Environmental regulations: Environment Protection Act, 1986
    • Compliance, risk & penalties: SEBI BRSR
    • Case examples: HUL/ITC (EPR + Plastic Waste Rules), Maruti/Tata Motors (BS6 compliance-led innovation), Tata Steel/JSW (industrial pollution compliance), Swiggy/Zomato (packaging regulation impact), UltraTech (waste co-processing)

    How companies leverage the CSR Act to build employee and community engagement?

    • CSR Act: fundamentals & compliance overview
    • CSR as a tool for brand trust and long-term goodwill
    • Employee engagement through CSR: volunteering, ownership, culture
    • Community engagement and partnerships: NGOs, local stakeholders
    • Impact measurement and storytelling: outcomes, credibility

    How to communicate with clarity and confidence as a leader?

    • Public speaking fundamentals
    • Influence and persuasion
    • Business writing
    • Presentation and storytelling
    • Negotiations

    The Policy Playbook Challenge:

    Step into the role of national policymakers and draft a future ready sustainability or business regulation. Diagnose a sector level market failure, design incentives, penalties and compliance, build a data backed policy brief, and assess industry, stakeholder, economic and environmental outcomes.

    Carbon Markets & Climate Finance

    Explore how carbon is measured, traded, financed, and monetized—and how climate-focused businesses and startups are built around it.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How carbon markets function?

    • Compliance vs voluntary carbon markets
    • Carbon credit lifecycle: creation, verification, issuance, retirement
    • Carbon credit pricing drivers
    • Credit quality and integrity checks

    How climate tech startups scale?

    • Venture capital landscape for climate tech
    • Disruptive startup models across industries: mobility, energy, recycling
    • Required ecosystem enablers: regulations, infrastructure, policy support
    • Case examples: Sun Mobility (battery swapping), ReNew (renewables scale + climate finance), Watershed/Persefoni (carbon accounting SaaS), Ather Energy (premium hardware + software integration), Avaada / Adani Green (platform-scale renewable infrastructure)

    How businesses finance climate initiatives?

    • Decarbonization and its ROI
    • Investment areas: renewable energy, storage, efficiency, supply chain diagnostics
    • Climate finance instruments: green bonds, sustainability-linked loans (SLLs)
    • Large investment players and their objectives

    How companies measure their carbon footprint?

    • Carbon footprint relevance for business decisions: GHG Protocol
    • Scope 1, 2, 3 emissions measurement and classification
    • Emissions hotspots across operations and supply chains
    • Carbon measurement metrics and ESG disclosures

    How investors evaluate and fund climate tech businesses?

    • How climate tech investing differs from traditional tech
    • Risk vs return in climate investments: technology, policy, infrastructure risks
    • Long gestation periods and patient capital
    • What VCs, PE funds, and infrastructure funds look for
    • Unit economics vs climate impact trade-offs
    • Role of government-backed funds and DFIs
    • Case examples: Breakthrough Energy Ventures, TPG Rise, Temasek, BlackRock Climate Funds

    Sustainable Investment Challenge:

    Act as the ESG team for a listed style company and build a credible 2030 sustainability roadmap. Benchmark peers, identify material ESG risks and value levers, design net zero, circularity and Scope 3 strategies, define finance linked KPIs, and model emissions reduction pathways.

    Renewable Energy Businesses

    Understand how the renewable energy sector works—from technology and business models to project economics and scaling strategies.

    • In Class
    • Out Class
    • How does the renewable energy industry work?
    • How leading renewable companies win?
    • Which new renewable business models are scaling?
    • What technology powers renewable energy?
    • How do renewable energy projects make money?

    How the renewable energy ecosystem works?

    • India’s growth in the renewable energy sector and its potential
    • Market overview across sectors: solar, wind, hydro, bioenergy
    • Key demand drivers: policy push, corporate net-zero, cost competitiveness
    • Renewable value chain and ecosystem players: OEMs, EPCs, developers, DISCOMs

    Who are the leading renewable companies and how are they winning?

    • Scaling renewable companies across two markets: large grid-scale power plants and distributed rooftop installations
    • Corporate clean energy adoption examples: RE100 companies such as Google, Apple, Microsoft
    • Common execution challenges and failure points
    • Case examples: Adani Green, ReNew, Tata Power Renewables, NTPC Green; NextEra, Enel

    Which technological disruptions are driving the sector forward?

    • Rooftop solar for homes and SMEs: sales, installation, EMI financing models
    • Commercial & industrial (C&I) renewable contracts: green power procurement for cost savings and ESG goals
    • Hybrid projects: solar + wind + storage for stable power
    • Battery storage as a service: storing energy and supplying during peak demand
    • Grid support services: balancing supply and demand using storage and forecasting

    Which key problems need to be addressed for India to lead in renewables?

    • Solar and wind technology fundamentals
    • Storage and battery systems
    • Grid balancing and load management
    • Forecasting and dispatch systems

    How do renewable energy projects make money?

    • Power sale contracts and pricing: tariffs, PPAs, open access
    • Setup and operating cost drivers: equipment, land, operations
    • Output and performance drivers: availability, curtailment
    • Funding and scale mechanics: project finance, refinancing
    • Risk management: regulatory, counterparty, execution

    Sustainability Reporting War Room:

    Operate as a corporate sustainability team under scrutiny and produce an investor ready ESG report. Analyse fragmented data, flag greenwashing risks, conduct materiality, build BRSR Core, GRI and ISSB aligned dashboards, and draft transparent narratives linking ESG to governance and risk.

    Circular Economy & Waste

    Learn how waste, recycling, and circular systems become profitable businesses through smart design, operations, and market structures.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How to move businesses from linear to circular models?

    • Circular principles: reduce, reuse, recycle, redesign
    • Product redesign for circularity
    • Value loops: repair, refill, resale, take-back
    • Cost structures and unit economics
    • Profitability and scale levers

    How circular startups scale to success?

    • MVP design and validation
    • Go-to-market scale strategy: B2B contracts, municipal tie-ups, enterprise clients
    • Market validation and pricing fit
    • Case examples: Saahas Zero Waste, Ramky Enviro; Veolia, TerraCycle

    How packaging and recycling systems work?

    • Packaging material choices and cost trade-offs
    • Collection and segregation realities: informal and formal systems
    • Recycling value chain: sorting → processing → re-use
    • Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) compliance and producer responsibility models
    • Business models in waste management: aggregators, recyclers, brand tie-ups
    • Case examples: Saahas Zero Waste, Bisleri – Bottles for Change

    How technology enables circular economy businesses?

    • Role of technology in tracking waste and materials
    • Digital traceability: QR codes, barcodes, material passports
    • Sorting and processing technology: AI sorting, sensors, robotics (high-level)
    • Case examples: Recykal, Saahas Zero Waste tech stack, Greyparrot (AI sorting)

    How circular economy businesses generate revenue and scale sustainably?

    • Where circular value gets created
    • Revenue streams and monetization
    • Operating model that drives profitability
    • Scaling playbooks and expansion strategy
    • Case examples: Saahas Zero Waste, Veolia, TerraCycle

    Waste to Wealth Innovation Lab:

    Design profitable circular economy ventures using real waste streams. Map the waste lifecycle, build upcycling or recycling solutions, create circular revenue and unit economics, quantify carbon, waste and social impact, and evaluate feasibility, risks and scalability.

    Sustainable Logistics & Supply Chains

    Understand how modern supply chains are designed, digitized, optimized, and governed to reduce cost, risk, and environmental impact.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How modern supply chains are designed for sustainability?

    • Value chain mapping: end-to-end flow of goods
    • Supply chain structure: supplier tiers, hubs, warehouses
    • Resilience vs efficiency trade-offs
    • Case examples: Amazon fulfilment networks, Walmart retail supply chains

    How sustainable logistics is implemented in real companies?

    • Supplier coordination and on-time delivery systems
    • Packaging optimization and shipment consolidation
    • Reverse logistics: returns, repairs, recycling flows
    • Case examples: Unilever distribution, Amazon returns, Walmart logistics scale

    How innovative logistics models are reshaping supply chains?

    • On-demand vs contract logistics models
    • Platform-based logistics: Uber-like models for freight
    • Hyperlocal and dark-store models
    • Micro-fulfilment and decentralized warehousing
    • Shared logistics and asset pooling
    • D2C and last-mile innovation
    • Case examples: Zepto, Porter, Shadowfax, Delhivery, Amazon Flex

    How digital tools create supply chain transparency?

    • Traceability systems: barcode, QR, RFID basics
    • Real-time tracking and monitoring: movement, temperature, delays
    • Dashboards and control towers: decision visibility
    • Case examples: Amazon tracking systems, Unilever traceability initiatives

    How sustainable logistics businesses make money?

    • Logistics business models: 3PL, 4PL, asset-heavy vs asset-light
    • Revenue streams: per shipment, per km, per pallet, subscriptions, contracts
    • Pricing logic: distance, volume, speed, reliability
    • Cost structures: fuel, warehousing, labor, technology, compliance
    • Profitability drivers and margin pressures
    • Case examples: Delhivery, Blue Dart, DHL, Amazon Logistics

    Green Supply Chain Rearchitecture Challenge:

    Act as operations and sustainability leaders rearchitecting supply chains for low carbon scale with a 30% emissions cut target. Map hotspots, redesign packaging, energy and logistics, evaluate EVs, rail and low carbon 3PLs, align suppliers through KPIs and contracts, and quantify carbon plus cost impact.

    Sustainability in Action: Sector Labs

    Students choose one sector and deep dive into real-world sustainability challenges, industry dynamics, and solution-building.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    Electric Mobility & Transport

    How to understand and navigate the EV ecosystem in India?

    • Industry landscape and market evolution
    • Key players across OEMs, battery, charging & policy
    • EV value chain and infrastructure dynamics

    How to evaluate sustainability in mobility business models?

    • Real-world EV case study
    • Emerging mobility and charging innovations
    • Policy incentives and regulatory impact

    How to design a scalable sustainable mobility solution?

    • Identifying infrastructure gaps
    • Technology and cost considerations
    • Building an implementable mobility concept

    Construction & Real Estate

    How to decarbonise one of the world’s most emission-intensive sectors?

    • Industry landscape and emissions footprint
    • Regulatory and green building frameworks
    • Key ecosystem players

    How to integrate sustainability into real estate strategy?

    • Green developers and market leaders
    • Decarbonisation case study
    • Materials, design and lifecycle thinking

    How to design future-ready sustainable infrastructure?

    • Urban development challenges
    • Climate-resilient construction models
    • Building a sustainable infrastructure concept

    Textiles & Apparel

    How to transform global fashion supply chains for sustainability?

    • Industry landscape and sourcing ecosystems
    • Environmental and social risks in apparel
    • Leading brands and circular pioneers

    How to build circularity into fashion business models?

    • Circular fashion case study
    • Innovative materials and resale platforms
    • Waste and water reduction strategies

    How to create a viable circular fashion concept?

    • Sustainable sourcing strategy
    • Brand positioning and transparency
    • Designing a circular apparel solution

    Pharmaceuticals & Healthcare

    How to embed sustainability in a highly regulated industry?

    • Industry landscape and regulatory ecosystem
    • Pharma manufacturing and supply chain structures
    • Compliance-driven sustainability frameworks

    How to balance innovation, compliance and environmental impact?

    • Sustainable operations case study
    • Waste, energy and water management in pharma
    • ESG in healthcare delivery

    How to design a sustainable healthcare model?

    • Responsible manufacturing
    • Ethical supply chains
    • Patient-centric sustainability innovation

    FMCG & Consumer Goods

    How to rethink consumption in high-volume industries?

    • Industry landscape and consumption patterns
    • Major brands and distribution ecosystems
    • Environmental footprint of FMCG

    How to build sustainable packaging and circular systems?

    • Sustainable packaging case study
    • Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)
    • Circular and responsible consumption models

    How to design a responsible consumer brand?

    • Sustainable sourcing and materials
    • Distribution innovation
    • Building a sustainable FMCG concept

    Industry Immersion Internship:

    Step into the sustainability, ESG, climate tech, consulting, or operations function of a real organisation through a structured internship. Work on live business problems such as ESG reporting, decarbonisation roadmaps, circular economy pilots, policy implementation, or supply chain optimisation.

    Careers, Consulting & Ventures

    Prepare for the real world with career planning, job readiness, consulting-style problem solving, and startup & investor fundamentals.

    • In Class
    • Out Class

    How to plan your career and position yourself for the market?

    • Understanding career paths: corporate, consulting, startups, founder, policy, operations
    • Identifying personal strengths, skills, and role fit
    • Building a clear professional narrative
    • Short-term vs long-term career planning
    • Industry role mapping and expectations

    How to crack jobs?

    • Resume building: role-specific, impact-focused
    • LinkedIn and personal branding
    • Portfolio building: projects, case work, live work
    • Interview preparation: HR and role-based
    • Mock interviews and feedback loops

    How professionals solve real-world business problems?

    • Structured thinking and problem breakdown
    • MECE logic and hypothesis building
    • Data interpretation and insights
    • Recommendation building
    • Client-style presentations
    • Case interview basics

    How to go from idea to business?

    • Identifying real problems worth solving
    • Idea validation and MVP thinking
    • Customer discovery
    • Basic business model design
    • Pricing, unit economics, and viability
    • Founder mindset and execution discipline

    How investors evaluate ideas and founders?

    • How investors think: VCs, angels, PE
    • What makes a business investable
    • Pitch deck structure
    • Storytelling for fundraising
    • Traction, metrics, and narratives
    • Mock pitch sessions

    Career Acceleration & Venture Sprint:

    Convert your internship experience into career outcomes. Refine your professional narrative, build role-ready resumes and portfolios, and prepare for interviews across corporate, consulting, startup, and policy roles. For aspiring founders, develop a venture thesis or startup idea grounded in real market problems, validate assumptions, build early traction or MVPs, and pitch to industry experts and investors.

    3+1 Dual Degree with
    Illinois Institute of Technology, US

    Discover how 3+1 Dual Degree Programme with Illinois Tech enables our
    students to study abroad & earn a globally recognised bachelor’s.

    Immerse Yourself in the Global Climate Economy

    Experience sustainability, policy, and green business firsthand through
    Global Immersion Programmes across leading climate and finance hubs.

    Where You’ll Go

    Europe

    UN, Rabobank, and circular-economy hubs to explore climate diplomacy, sustainable finance, carbon markets, and green manufacturing.

    Singapore

    Learn from Temasek, Singtel, & Singapore’s smart-city ecosystem on urban decarbonisation, net-zero infra, clean mobility, & ASEAN green investment.

    Japan

    Visit Rakuten, Nissan, & Kyoto Research Park to study hydrogen, EVs, resource efficiency & Japan’s circular-economy frameworks.

    Dubai

    Engage with DIFC, ADGM, and climate investors on sustainable finance, low-carbon cities, water innovation, and Middle East climate strategy.

    Real Conversations on Sustainability and Business

    Gain on-the-ground insights from faculty, founders, and young innovators building businesses aligned with sustainability.

    AI, Genetics, Robotics & Climate Tech

    Explore frontier tech reshaping climate solutions, green jobs & the SDG economy.

    Can This Gen Z Fix India’s Water Crisis?

    See a Gen Z-led venture tackle India’s water crisis by linking local innovation to climate resilience.

    Mamaearth: Building a Conscious Brand

    How a consumer brand scales with ethical sourcing and responsible consumption.

    Building the Future of EVs in India

    How EV founders navigate policy, infra, and capital to grow clean mobility jobs.

    Banjara Market: Livelihoods & Regulation

    What happens when urban policy hits informal markets supporting thousands of livelihoods.

    Tesla’s Crisis Communication Playbook

    How leaders communicate under pressure across media and stakeholders.

    Real Insights from
    Our Faculty

    Gain insights from industry leaders on how sustainability is shaping the future!

    Enrollment Checklist
    Admissions Criteria

    01.
    Internship & Work Experience

    01.
    Internship & Work Experience

    Experience in sustainability, climate action, consulting, policy, development sector, operations, supply chain, etc., that shows leadership, problem-solving, & execution.

    02.
    Academic & Co-Curricular Profile

    02.
    Academic & Co-Curricular Profile

    Undergraduate academic performance (any discipline), plus relevant certifications, projects, research, volunteering, sustainability initiatives, or entrepreneurial experience

    03.
    Communication & Leadership

    03.
    Communication & Leadership

    Ability to articulate purpose with clarity of thought, demonstrate professional presence, and show motivation to build a career at the intersection of business and sustainability

    Application
    Timeline & Process

    Rounds Application Fees Status Action
    Round 1 INR 500/- Active Apply Now Apply Now
    Round 1 15th Oct ’25 -
    5th Dec ‘25    		 Nov/Dec ‘25 Dec/Jan ‘25 14th
    Jan '26     		INR 2,400/- Closed Aug '26 Apply Now
    Round 2 Dec '24 - 28th Feb '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
    Round 3 15th Mar '25 - 14th May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 Mar/Apr/May '25 INR 3,500/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now
    Round 4 18th May '25 - 16th Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 Jun/Jul '25 INR 4,000/- Closed Aug '25 Apply Now

    01.

    Application
    Submission

    01.

    Application
    Submission

    Requirements

    Share Your Background

    Begin by filling out the online application form with your academic history, professional experience (if any), and motivations for pursuing sustainability and business.

    You will need:

    • Academic transcripts (Bachelor’s degree in any discipline)

    • Updated CV with internships, projects, or work experience

    • Any projects, initiatives, or achievements that strengthen your profile
      (e.g., campus clubs, entrepreneurship, sustainability/ESG work, research)

    This helps the admissions committee understand your readiness for a rigorous, 12-month programme and your potential to grow into a sustainability-focused business leader.

    02.

    Video Essay

    02.

    Video Essay

    Requirements

    Share Your Story, Motivation & Sustainability Interests

    After submitting your application form, you will submit a 2-minute video essay introducing yourself, your interests, and why you should be selected for the programme. You may also speak about why you are passionate about sustainability and the problem you want to solve or address early in your career.

    • Quantitative reasoning

    • Logical and analytical thinking

    • Data interpretation

    • Basic problem-solving under time pressure

    Please note: your video should be uploaded to Google Drive and uploaded in part 2 of your application form. Kindly ensure that the video on Google Drive is set to "Anyone with the link can view".

    You will also be required to submit a Letter of Recommendation (LOR) from an academic faculty member, internship manager, or professional supervisor. The LOR should highlight your strengths, work ethic, and readiness to work on sustainability, ESG, climate, or impact-oriented projects in a rigorous, fast-paced programme.

    03.

    Sustainability &
    Business
    Interview

    03.

    Sustainability & Business Interview

    Requirements

    Deep-Dive into Your Thinking, Potential & Fit

    Shortlisted applicants will proceed to a personal interview, conducted by Masters’ Union faculty, sustainability practitioners, or business leaders.

    You’ll be assessed on:

    • How you structure and solve real-world business problems

    • Comfort with numbers, trade-offs, and ambiguity

    • Awareness of sustainability / climate trends and their business impact

    • Your motivation to build a career in the climate & SDG economy

    • Coachability, communication, and leadership potential

    Interviews may include short case discussions, questions on your past experiences, and scenario-based conversations around ESG, policy, or sustainable ventures

    04.

    Admission
    Decision

    04.

    Admission Decision

    Requirements

    Status of Evaluation

    The admissions committee takes a holistic view of your candidacy, looking at your application form and academic background, your performance in the aptitude test, feedback from your personal interview, your overall fit for a fast-paced 12-month sustainability and business programme, and any additional materials you may have submitted (such as a video essay or portfolio).

    Applicants will be placed into one of three categories:

    Accepted

    Candidates offered admission will receive:

    • Their admission decision

    • Details of programme fee and any scholarship awarded

    • A deadline (typically 7–21 days) to confirm their seat by paying the admission fee.

    Waitlisted

    • Masters’ Union does not maintain a ranked waitlist. Waitlisted applicants may be offered admission if seats open up in the cohort. The committee reconvenes regularly to review profiles and move strong candidates from the waitlist to the accepted pool.

    Not Selected

    • Applicants who are not selected are welcome to reapply for a future cohort, especially after strengthening their profile through additional work experience, projects, or academic performance.

    Fee Structure

    Fee Timelines Amount
    		 Due Date
    Admission Offer Acceptance INR 75,000/- Before 31st May, 2026
    Programme Commencement INR 10,95,000/- Before start of classes
    Mid-Programme Installment INR 10,95,000/- After 3 months
    Total INR 22,65,000/-

    Investing in the
    Future of Our Students

    Merit-Based Scholarships

    Scholarships awarded to learners who demonstrate exceptional academic strength, professional experience, and technical aptitude.

    Need-Based Scholarships

    Support for candidates facing financial constraints, ensuring talent is never limited by affordability.

    Explore Programme

    Frequently Asked
    Questions

    • General
    • Eligibility
    • Application Queries
    • Admission Policies
    • Immersion

    What does a typical day look like for an SBM student?

    Students attend 2–3 instructor-led classes, participate in hands-on sustainability projects, and engage in CXO talks, industry practicums, SDG-focused workshops, and career counselling sessions. The day blends business learning with real-world sustainability exposure.

    Can applicants tailor their Masters’ Union SBM programme?

    Yes. SBM offers a wide range of sustainability, ESG, climate-tech, and circular economy electives. You can choose modules and immersion experiences that align with your career goals while still earning the same SBM certification.

    Are the Scholarships the Same for PGP TBM and PGP SBM?

    Yes, the scholarships available for both the PGP TBM and the PGP SBM are the same. For detailed information on the scholarship options, please refer to our admissions page.

    How are students graded in SBM?

    Evaluation is based on class participation, quizzes, projects, group assignments, fieldwork, and exams. Grading follows a standard normal curve (Z-curve), and students must maintain a minimum GPA of 2.1 to graduate.

    What are the key benefits of the 12-month SBM programme?

    The SBM is an accelerated, high-impact programme featuring:

    • 9+ months of academic and project-based learning

    • 3-month internship or industry immersion

    • Exposure to global sustainability hubs (US, UK, Europe, Singapore)

    • Strong pathways into careers in ESG, climate finance, clean-tech, impact consulting, and sustainable operations

    Is SBM suitable for students without an environment or business background?

    Yes. The programme includes foundational courses in sustainability, business, climate science, and analytics. Students from any academic background can excel with support from faculty, mentors, and hands-on learning opportunities.

    What is the typical student profile for the SBM programme?

    SBM attracts students from diverse academic and professional backgrounds—business, engineering, humanities, environmental science, finance and more.

    If I am completing my undergraduate studies, is SBM right for me?

    Yes. Fresh graduates can apply to the 12-month SBM programme. While prior work experience is helpful, it is not mandatory, and applicants who have completed or are completing their undergraduate degree are fully eligible.

    What's the minimum qualifying GPA?

    Masters’ Union does not have a minimum GPA requirement as the GPA system is not a global grading methodology. We focus on the courses students have taken, grades received and overall academic performance.

    Is there an age limit for entry?

    No, there is no age limit for the SBM programme. Anyone who has completed their undergraduate degree — whether a fresher or a working professional — is eligible to apply. If you have significant work experience and want clarity on fit, you can reach out to the admissions team at admissions@mastersunion.org for personalised guidance.

    Is there an age limit for entry?

    There is no age limit for entry. However, the programme is most suitable for those who are at an early stage in their careers. If students have more work experience, we encourage them to get in touch with the admissions team at admissions@mastersunion.org , who will evaluate their profile and advise them accordingly.

    If applicants don’t have a business background, will it affect my chances of admission?

    Not at all. The SBM programme welcomes applicants from all academic fields — engineering, arts, law, social sciences, design, medicine, and more. What matters most is your interest in sustainability, your leadership potential, and your overall academic or project performance. A non-business background will not disadvantage your application.

    If an applicant doesn’t have an undergraduate degree, can they still be considered for admission?

    Yes! While the vast majority of our participants have undergraduate and/or advanced degrees, this is not compulsory for admission. Candidates who do not hold an undergraduate degree, but who have an excellent career record, examination scores and leadership potential are also welcome to apply. All those who will be completing their undergrad degree by May of the following year will be eligible to apply.

    Who are we looking for?

    We are looking for a candidate who uses available opportunities to the best of their ability, is curious to learn new things, remains open to opposing viewpoints and new ideas, and exhibits strong leadership performance and potential.

    Can applicants get a call back from Masters’ Union?

    Given the volume of requests, we’d encourage students to reach out to the admissions team via email at pgadmissions@mastersunion.org. If students would still like to connect with us on call, reach out to our counselling team on WhatsApp using the following link.

    Can applicants defer their admission offer from Masters’ Union to the next year?

    Currently, we are not offering the option to defer admission. Those who cannot join the programme in the current year are advised to apply again next year.

    Are all applicants interviewed?

    Only candidates who have been shortlisted post their application submission will be invited for the personal interview.

    Are any of the rounds more or less competitive than others?

    Each round is equally competitive, with the same acceptance ratio across all rounds. The most important factor is to dedicate sufficient time and effort into your application to accurately showcase your strengths and talents. However, applying in Round 1 offers several advantages:

    • Scholarships: If scholarships are a priority, we encourage applying in Round 1.

    • Hostel Room Choice: Round 1 students get priority in selecting their hostel rooms, ensuring they can choose from the best available options.

    • Notice Period: They have ample time to serve notice periods at their current jobs.

    • Break Before Studies: They can take a well-deserved break before starting 16 months of intensive study.

    • Loan and Funding: They have more time to apply for loans and arrange funds.

    Can applicants get their profile evaluated?

    Feel free to connect with the admissions team to understand your fitment with the programme. While applicants are welcome to send their CV to admissions@mastersunion.org for a preliminary conversation, due to a large volume of requests, our responses might be delayed.

    Does Masters’ Union have a cut-off for MU-BAAT?

    Masters’ Union follows a holistic evaluation approach for admissions and does not have a cut-off for the MU-BAAT. Our selection process considers a combination of academic and professional experience, communication skills, and other application components.

    Do you accept GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition scores?

    Yes, we do accept both GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition scores. However, If you feel your GMAT or GMAT Focus score does not reflect your true potential, we encourage you to take the MU-BAAT to strengthen your overall application.

    Is the same reporting policy applicable to both GMAT and GMAT Focus Edition? Will this impact application?

    Yes, the same reporting policy applies to both the GMAT Focus Edition and standard GMAT. Students will have the option to report the scores of both versions of the GMAT in our application form. The choice of taking the GMAT Focus Edition will not have any negative impact on the application. Note: The conversion table designed by GMAT, is to help students convert their GMAT Focus score to the equivalent standard GMAT score and vice versa. To convert their scores, students should locate their GMAT Focus score in the left column and find the corresponding standard GMAT score in the right column.

    Are there more details about the MU-BAAT?

    Yes, the MU-BAAT (Masters' Union Business Aptitude and Admissions Test) is a key component of the admissions process. After submitting your application form, you will be required to take the MU-BAAT.

    The MU-BAAT reflects our commitment to a fair and technology-driven admissions process, focusing entirely on your critical thinking skills and potential contributions to Masters' Union. No specific preparation is needed—just show up as your authentic self.

    MU-BAAT is optional for applicants who submit a valid CAT, GMAT, or GMAT Focus score. However, if you feel your GMAT or GMAT Focus score does not reflect your true potential, we encourage you to take the MU-BAAT to strengthen your overall application.

    Application Submission Policy

    If the applicant misses all the three rounds of a particular intake, the application form and its entire content will expire. At this point, the applicant will be required to complete a new application from scratch, for the next intake. An application will only be evaluated once it is complete. If it is completed after the deadline, it will be reviewed for the next round or (in the case of last round applicants) the following intake.

    Hostel Policy

    Masters’ Union offers opt-in accommodation to students admitted to the PGP TBM. We offer a variety of options, each close to our campus and equipped with amenities to ensure a comfortable living experience. The fees for the course, as mentioned on the website, do not include hostel fees. The full fee structure, including that of the hostel, is sent to all our admitted students along with their offer letter.

    Feedback Policy

    Due to the large number of applications we receive for each intake, and the confidentiality of the deliberations of the admissions committee, we would be unable to provide detailed individual feedback on unsuccessful applications.

    Refund Policy

    Fees once submitted cannot be refunded. Masters’ Union reserves the right to amend this Refund Policy at any time without prior notice.

    Is participation in immersions mandatory or optional?

    Participation in any immersion is optional and depends on the student’s interest. Since our immersions are theme-based, students can choose the one that best aligns with their career goals and learning preferences.

    Is the immersion fee included in the tuition fee?

    The immersion fee is not included in the tuition fee. It is a separate, optional cost applicable only to students who choose to participate in the Global or Bharat Immersion programs. 1 day off campus immersions are part of your program with no added fee.

    What is the cost for participating in the immersion programs?

    The international immersions range between ₹2.5–3 lakhs, while the National Immersion is priced at approximately ₹6 lakhs for the full program. There are limited seats we encourage students to block their participation as early as possible.

    Can students choose between Global and National Immersion options?

    Yes, students can choose between Global and National Immersion options based on their interests, career goals, and preferred learning experiences.

    Are there scholarships or financial aid options available?

    Yes - we offer scholarships on a first-come, first-served basis for select immersions.

    Will there be any certification or academic recognition after completion?

    Yes! For international immersions, students receive an official certificate from the host university upon successful completion of the program. For the Bharat Immersion you will receive an official certificate from Masters’ Union.

    What kind of networking opportunities are available during the immersion?

    The immersions are designed to provide students with ample networking opportunities with faculty and peers at the host university, as well as with industry professionals and leaders during company visits and interactions.

    Are there any financing options?

    Yes, there are financial institutions that can provide loans for up to 95% of the programme fees to the admitted students. Read here for further details.

    Is the admissions process different for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?

    No, the admissions process for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme is the same as the standard process. Applicants must apply through the regular admissions portal, and eligibility for the programme is assessed based on their academic performance, extracurricular activities, and an interview.

    Will students have the option to choose the duration of transfer?

    Currently, the programme is designed as a one-year transfer option. Students will spend their fourth year at Illinois Institute of Technology, completing the final year of their undergraduate degree as part of the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme.

    When can the student decide if they want to opt for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme with Illinois Institute of Technology?

    Students are strongly encouraged to decide on opting for the 3+1 Dual Degree UG Programme before starting their undergraduate journey at Masters’ Union. Early confirmation ensures guaranteed seat allocation and allows for streamlined academic planning tailored to the programme's requirements.

    While students may still choose to opt for the programme during their third year, this is subject to seat availability, which will be communicated at that time. Confirming at the beginning ensures a smooth transition and optimal preparation for their transfer to Illinois Tech.

    Will the curriculum at Illinois Tech align with what students study at Masters’ Union?

    Yes, the curriculum has been designed to ensure a seamless transfer between the institutions. Students will continue to build on the foundational and advanced topics they study at Masters’ Union.

    Can students secure internships in the US while at Illinois Tech?

    Yes, students will have access to Illinois Tech’s career services, which include internship placements, job fairs, and networking events with global employers.

    What will I graduate with at the end of the programme?

    Upon completing the programme, you will be awarded a Professional Certificate for Undergraduate Programme in Tech & Business Management by Masters' Union. Additionally, you will have the option to enrol in IIM Bangalore to pursue a BBA in Digital Business and Entrepreneurship, provided you clear all the exams and the eligibility criteria as set by the IIM Bangalore.

    What is the difference between the Masters' Union certification and the IIM Bangalore degree?

    The Masters' Union certification focuses on an industry-integrated, tech-driven curriculum designed for business management professionals. The IIM Bangalore (BBA) degree is a traditional academic qualification offered with standard coursework and university examinations.

    What makes the Masters’ Union Programme a better choice than earning the same degree from IIM Bangalore?

    While the IIM Bangalore degree provides a strong academic foundation,Masters' Union offers real-world exposure from day one. Through outclasses and internships starting in Year 1, students gain hands-on experience with industry leaders from top companies like Microsoft, Google, and Zomato. This early exposure, combined with rigorous academic training, ensures that our students are not only academically strong but also industry-ready, with the highest CTC touching ₹61.5 LPA and an average CTC of ₹28.5 LPA, securing top-tier job offers even before graduation.

    Is this an online degree, is it authentic?

    Yes, the degree is awarded through IIM Bangalore’s online platform, making it an online degree. However, it holds the same recognition and value as any full-time IIM Bangalore degree.

    How well is this degree recognised for higher studies abroad?

    The degree awarded by IIM Bangalore is highly respected and recognised for higher studies abroad. With IIM Bangalore’s global reputation and the rigor of its curriculum, the degree holds significant international credibility, opening doors to top global universities and MBA programs for further education.

    Can I still pursue the Masters' Union certification if I don’t enrol in the IIM Bangalore degree?

    Yes, you can still complete the Masters' Union Undergraduate Programme in Tech and Business Management and earn the certification even if you choose not to enrol in the IIM Bangalore degree.
